shots.line: Right now, sitting perfectly still, you feel like nothing is moving at all. That feeling is the greatest illusion of your life.

shots.title: how fast is fast
shots.line: We think we know what fast means. A sprinter hits forty-four kilometers an hour. A jet, nine hundred. A rifle bullet, three thousand four hundred.
But the ground beneath your feet is not still either. Our planet is spinning, and at the equator it turns at sixteen hundred and seventy kilometers an hour.

shots.label: the earth's spin
shots.line: That is half the speed of a bullet. And you never feel a thing.
Now pull back, and watch what our whole planet is really doing.

shots.label: earth around the sun
shots.line: Earth is falling around the Sun at one hundred and seven thousand kilometers an hour. Thirty kilometers every single second.

shots.statement: And the Sun itself is not still — it falls around the heart of the galaxy, dragging every planet with it.
shots.line: And the Sun itself is not still. It falls around the center of the galaxy, dragging every planet with it.

shots.label: our speed through the galaxy
shots.line: Eight hundred and twenty-eight thousand kilometers an hour.
A tiny world, riding a star, around a galaxy that is itself racing through the universe.

shots.statement: You have never been still — not for one single second of your life.
shots.line: You have never been still. Not for one single second of your life.
The stillness you feel isn't stillness at all. It is just the speed of everything, moving together.
